case_title: MONTENEGRO VS MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIF

> TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS
STIPULATION AND ORDER TO FILE BRIEF IN OPPOSITION TO WRIT PETITION IN EXCESS OF PAGE LIMIT
MINI-MINUTES FOR FEB-06-2004 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PETITIONER MONTENEGRO, JOSE RECOVER FROM RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA SEE SCANNED DOC: AS TO LEGAL CONCLUSION 10, 2ND PARAGRAPH, 2ND SENTENCE ONLY; IN ALL OTHER RESPECTS, THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS IS DENIED. NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT GRANTING IN PART AND DENYING IN PART PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS FILED BY RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY PETITIONER MONTENEGRO, JOSE HEARING SET FOR FEB-06-2004 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301
STIOPULATION TO FILE BRIEF IN OPPOSITION TO WRIT PETITION IN EXCESS OF PAGE LIMIT FILED BY RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER JOSE MONTENEGRO'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS: THE COURT REVERSES, AS AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION, THE MEDICAL BOARD'S FINDING, PURSUANT TO BUSINESS & PROFESSION CODE 822, THAT PETITIONER SUFFERS FROM A MEDICAL IMPAIRMENT THAT, BY ITSELF, INHIBITS HIS ABILITY TO PRACTICE MEDICINE. A WRIT OF MANDATE SHALL ISSUE. THE COURT REMANDS THIS ISSUE ONLY TO THE MEDICAL BOARD TO SET ASIDE ITS DECISION AND ISSUE A NEW DECISION IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE JUDGMENT OF THIS COURT. THE COURT, HOWEVER, FINDS THAT BUSINESS & PROFESSION CODE 2241, 2242 AND 2234 FINDINGS BY THE MEDICAL BOARD ARE SUPPORTED BY THE RECORD AND THERE IS NO ABUSE OF DISCRETION. PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR STATEMENT OF DECISION IS DENIED PURSUANT TO 632 CCP(ORAL RECORD). ORDER RETURNING ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD SIGNED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558
REPLY MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS
PEITION FOR WIRT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Feb-06-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Albert J. Garcia, Esq., (510) 848-5190, for Petitioner and Moving Party Montenegro
Heidi Weisbaum, Deputy Attorney General (619) 645-2098, for Respondent and Opposing Party Medical Board of California
RULING:
ARGUED. Petitioner Jose Montenegro's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus: The Court reverses, as an abuse of discretion, the Medical Board's finding, pursuant to Business & Profession Code 822, that Petitioner suffers from a medical impairment that, by itself, inhibits his ability to practice medicine. A writ of mandate shall issue. The Court remands this issue only to the Medical Board to set aside its decision and issue a new decision in accordance with the judgment of this Court. The Court, however, finds that Business & Profession Code 2241, 2242 and 2234 findings by the Medical Board are supported by the record and there is no abuse of discretion. Petitioner's Request For Statement Of Decision is DENIED pursuant to 632 CCP(oral record). Order Returning Administrative Record signed by the Court. (301/JLW/DS)
